author: @ilhadad
text:
All, thanks for all your videos and encouraging investigation and inventiveness.
I don't have a budget to be buying lab equipment so, like you guys, I have been making my own as I go along.  My question is have you guys used a dremel as a homogenizer? It has very high RPM's and given that I too am in the process of building my own super cap's I thought that instead of a blender I could use a dremel with the press jig you guys made.  Any thoughts, suggestion or advice?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: we hve never tried it mate but it sounds like a good idea - fit is going to be the real issue - if you have a lather it's probably doable - bu without one - i think it would be quite a challenge

replies.author: @ilhadad
replies.text:
Let me give it a shot.  I'll post the results.  Send my regards to Steve for showing us these tools.

I wish I had money to buy an industrial high pressure homogenizer (I used to work in a manufacturing facility)  Those things are great they would shear graphite without an issue.  Unfortunately they are pricey.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: they are a bit and you are quite right they would work great lol

author: @eeezygoing
text: Thanks Steve for the video. It is very well done and great to see the way you do things in the workshop. Edward

replies.author: @fwgsteve3806
replies.text: eeezygoing Thanks Edward. I'm quite keen to show behind the scenes stuff.... The triumphs and disasters!!!

author: @christheother9088
text: Yes, I must get one of those drill press vices.  I occaisonally make bad decisons around drill presses.

replies.author: @fwgsteve3806
replies.text: ***** I know what you mean. I went for one as soon as I got down to three usable fingers. :)

author: @littlestworkshop
text: If you must store your chuck key in the chuck, pop it in the jaws.

replies.author: @fwgsteve3806
replies.text: ***** You're absolutely right. Good workshop practice. We do have a safety guard on this particular machine and it would not operate with the key where it is.
